title: The Twentieth Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## October 7, 2018 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

October 7, 2018 was the Twentieth Sunday after Pentecost in the Christian calendar, placing the sermon in Ordinary Time following Pentecost. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would have included Job 1:1, 2:1-10 (the suffering of Job) and Mark 10:2-16 (Jesus on divorce and children), with Psalm 26 and 2 Timothy 1:1-14 as supporting texts. This Sunday fell just before the beginning of the Christmas/Advent preparation season, roughly midway through the liturgical year.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the aftermath of its 79th General Convention held in Austin, Texas in July 2018, which had addressed marriage equality and transgender inclusion issues. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Robert J. Wright Jr., was navigating post-convention implementation of these resolutions. No major diocesan leadership changes or controversies were documented specifically around early October 2018.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced typical early autumn weather in the first week of October 2018, with temperatures in the 60s-70s Fahrenheit. The Colorado midterm election campaign was intensifying with just over a month before the November 2018 elections, with major races including the gubernatorial race between Democrat Jared Polis and Republican Walker Stapleton generating significant local media coverage. No major weather disasters or community crises dominated Denver news during this specific week.

NATIONAL NEWS

The Senate Judiciary Committee was completing its investigation into Supreme Court nominee Brett Kavanaugh following Christine Blasey Ford's testimony about alleged sexual assault, with a full Senate vote scheduled for the following week. The Trump administration continued implementing tariffs and trade policies affecting Colorado's agricultural and manufacturing sectors. The 2018 midterm elections were ramping up as a major national focus with exactly one month remaining.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Saudi Arabian government was under international scrutiny regarding journalist Jamal Khashoggi's disappearance (he had vanished on October 2, with details emerging this week), which would dominate Middle East coverage. Brexit negotiations between the United Kingdom and European Union remained deadlocked over the Irish border issue, with tensions escalating in early October. Hurricane Michael was approaching the Gulf Coast as a major Category 4 storm, threatening Florida and the southeastern United States.

CULTURAL MOMENT

America was experiencing significant cultural division and anxiety heading into the midterm elections, with partisan polarization visibly high across media and social platforms. The #MeToo movement remained culturally prominent and politically salient given the Kavanaugh hearings dominating national conversation. Sports audiences were focused on the beginning of the NFL season (Denver Broncos playing) and Major League Baseball playoffs, with the World Series approaching.
